prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: staff
Channel scope: staff-handbook, policy-documents

Topic document:
### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

In the event of a serious injury or medical emergency, staff will immediately call emergency services (112) and follow the instructions provided by the professionals.

### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

Family/guardians will be contacted as soon as possible by the designated staff member. A member of school staff will accompany the child to the hospital if necessary and remain with the child until the parent/guardian arrives. (This applies also in the event of an accident abroad.)

### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

If the child has a fever or any other symptom that prevents them from enjoying the experience, the family will be contacted to pick them up as soon as possible.

### Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency > Procedure in Case of an Accident or Emergency

All families must agree to the pickup commitment before authorizing the outing.
raw_response: {"facts":[{"title":"Emergency call for serious injury","content":"Staff must immediately call emergency services by dialing 112 in the event of a serious injury or medical emergency and adhere to the instructions given by emergency professionals.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Contacting family or guardians","content":"A designated staff member must contact the family or guardians as soon as possible after an accident.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Accompanying child to hospital","content":"A member of the school staff must accompany the child to the hospital and remain with them until the parent or guardian arrives, even for accidents occurring abroad.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Pickup for fever or other symptoms","content":"If a child displays a fever or symptoms hindering their participation, the family must be contacted to collect the child as soon as possible.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]},{"title":"Pickup commitment agreement","content":"All families must agree to the pickup commitment prior to authorizing a child's participation in an outing.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["procedure-in-case-of-an-accident-or-emergency"]}]}

facts.title: Emergency call for serious injury
facts.content: Staff must immediately call emergency services by dialing 112 in the event of a serious injury or medical emergency and adhere to the instructions given by emergency professionals.

facts.title: Contacting family or guardians
facts.content: A designated staff member must contact the family or guardians as soon as possible after an accident.

facts.title: Accompanying child to hospital
facts.content: A member of the school staff must accompany the child to the hospital and remain with them until the parent or guardian arrives, even for accidents occurring abroad.

facts.title: Pickup for fever or other symptoms
facts.content: If a child displays a fever or symptoms hindering their participation, the family must be contacted to collect the child as soon as possible.

facts.title: Pickup commitment agreement
facts.content: All families must agree to the pickup commitment prior to authorizing a child's participation in an outing.
